title: Presidency University, Bangalore - Honest Review | All You Need To Know - Fees, Placements, Courses
body: I got mail from Christ University saying that the inauguration ceremony is on 26th July and that classes and orientation will follow. But since board marks are set to be declared before July 30, we're confused about whether or not we should retain our place in Christ (since we have other college options as well). So, if you can create a youtube video about when classes start and how to handle this situation, it will he super helpful for all of us.
author: @nishitha5151

title: What a day in the Life of a Police Cadet at the Academy looks like
body: The fact you were able to even film this shows me how relaxed this academy is lol. My academy wouldnt even let us have our phones on our person for the entire 21 weeks we were there. No hate or anything though, we all do the same job in the end so keep striving man
author: @dylanbradley7583

title: What a day in the Life of a Police Cadet at the Academy looks like
body: I graduated from the academy in 2011. Although I didn't get hired on with law enforcement, it was one of the best achievements in my life. Very proud of it.
author: @LiLgPnoy15

title: What a day in the Life of a Police Cadet at the Academy looks like
body: This academy is so relaxed man compared to mine. We got screamed and yelled at for the first two months then after a while we earned little privileges like snack time and stuff. I didn’t get to go out and get lunch at all we had to bring lunch we had no refrigerator or a microwave. Plus I had to shave my head and couldn’t start growing my hair back until the middle of the Academy but my hair couldn’t be but a inch and a half long military fade. I missed my waves and I’m still trying to get my waves back lol.
author: @jackwashington8664

title: What a day in the Life of a Police Cadet at the Academy looks like
body: Going to the academy on January 17th , Thanks to the lord! This video helped a lot bro. Keep doing what you do.
author: @Jeremyx21

title: What a day in the Life of a Police Cadet at the Academy looks like
body: Up @ 6.. I wish.. we had to be ready and in our seats by 0630, hair no longer than 1/4” , no facial hair.. no way a recording device would have been allowed in the building (phones and smart watches were put in envelopes and turned off until you leave) and eating and drinking in class.. other than the water bottle they gave us would be a BIG no no… everyone’s desks were expected to be organized the same way ( note books and pens in the same spot, water bottles all in the same place in the desks, exct) no leaving for lunch. And god for bid you cursed and an officer overheard it.. man everyone would be smoked. But good for you man.. not all academy’s are built the same.
author: @PatrickPesoli

title: What a day in the Life of a Police Cadet at the Academy looks like
body:
Honestly you have motivated me so
Much and I don’t regret my decision in becoming a police officer in the future.

title: What a day in the Life of a Police Cadet at the Academy looks like
body: Wow, never seen a non-stress academy before. In our academy, we'd assemble and march into the classrooms, subject to any scrutiny unless actually in classroom or C7, we were pretty much at some form of attention, all 26 weeks. And there was never such thing as "I don't plan on running."🤣

title: COLLEGE DORM TOUR 2018 | University of Richmond
body: I wanted to add a comment to this video now that I am a month out from graduation from UR. During my time here I have received an INCREDIBLE education, met great friends, and got to spend the best four months of my life studying abroad. However, I can’t ignore the blatant issues with the campus culture. Please look at the Instagram accounts @ abolishrichmondgreeklife and @ protectourweb to get an idea. The way UR has handled recent issues regarding racism is disgusting. It is great to see that many students have been pushing for positive change recently, but I want to make sure that anyone who is considering attending knows both the pros and cons of the school. I hope this can help add an additional perspective to your college decision process and feel free to reach out to me on Instagram with any questions!
author: @BWithEmMarie
